calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-09-292293 Conservatorship of Dotson & PTR-09-292738.
Appearances: Rowena Navia for Paula Bibbs.

Ms. Navia stated that she had nothing additional to report. The accounting petitions in both cases were granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Yeung. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-06-2015 Continued To May-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-11-2015 Continued To Apr-08-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1614 Quesada Ave., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for Paula Bibbs.

The sale was confirmed at $580,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $226,000.00 is required. Ms. Navia stated that the initial requested bond amount was reduced by the balance of the reverse mortgage and legal fees for Court appointed Counsel.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Sell Trust Real Property And For Authority To Enter Into An Exclusive Listing Agreement With Pat Rock, Coldwell Banker [Probate Code Section 17200 (B)(8)
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs. Franchesca Callejo court appointed Counsel for Lola Mae Dotson. Mark Cato in Propria Persona. Janice Mendenhall in Propria Persona. Johnny Hayes was also present but did not address the Court.

The Court stated that it appears the property needs to be sold because the reverse mortgage needs to be paid back. Ms. Navia stated that they have to inform the lender that Ms. Dotson is no longer residing in the property. Ms. Callejo stated that Ms. Dotson has been out of the house for quite some time, that Ms. Dotson is adjusted quite well at the facility and that she does not see any other alternative but to sell the house. Ms. Mendenhall stated that they never agreed to work for free, that they have some personal belongings in the house, that they need to get their belongings out and that they want to be paid for their time. Ms. Bibbs will have a designee meet Mr. Cato at the house on Saturday November 9, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. Mr. Cato is to have a police stand by and have his belongings out in three hours. Mr. Cato and Ms. Mendenhall are to provide a list of their belongings to Ms. Callejo by November 1, 2013. Ms. Callejo will facilitate communication. The Court suggested Ms. Bibbs take some photographs prior to any belongings being removed. The Court also informed Ms. Mendenhall and Mr. Cato that the claim for their time is not before the Court and that they will have to file a petition with approval from the Department of the Presiding Judge. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Sell Trust Real Property, etc. (order for the same signed).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number PCN-09-292293 Conservatorship of Lola Mae Dotson.

Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs. Lola Mae Dotson (Conservatee) also present. Mark Cato, Charline Dixon, Juanita Dixon, Chalic Adams and Janice Mendelhall were also present.

Ms. Navia stated that both of the accountings could be submitted. Ms. Bibbs stated that the loan was paid off once the reverse mortgage was completed. All Parties expressed their concerns, spoke of the issues between the Family members, and the issues between Ms. Bibbs and Mr. Cato. Some Parties presented support for the services Ms. Bibbs was providing to the Conservatee. The accountings were taken under submission and the Court stated that it was satisfied with how the Conservatorship is going.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-14-2010 Continued To Dec-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Continued To Dec-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Cato in Propria Persona. Janice Mendenhall in Propria Persona. Lola Mae Dotson also present.

This was pre-granted matter. Mr. Cato stated that he was objecting to the petition. The matter was continued to December 14, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Mr. Cato to file his objections.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Ex Parte Petition For Appointment Of Temporary Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with Paula Bibbs (Proposed Temporary Successor Trustee). Court-Appointed Counsel Mary Jane Gordon for and with Lola Mae Dotson (Trustor). Mark Cato (Nephew) in pro per.

Ms. Navia stated that they are petitioning due to a lack of funds, that there is an urgency because there are no funds in the Estate, that they are at a deficit at this point, that Ms. Bibbs has paid some of the expenses herself, that the papers should include a declination by Jesse Lott, that the other two have passed, and that it would be in the best interest of the Ms. Dotson to have Ms. Bibbs serve in both capacities. Ms. Gordon stated that she has discussed it with her client, that she is not sure that her client understands the urgency, and that she tried to assure her client that she would not lose her property. Mr. Cato stated his objections for the record. The Court stated that considering the representations by Ms. Navia and Ms. Gordon that it was granting the petition appointing Paula Bibbs as the Temporary Successor Trustee and that the Trust come under Court supervision. The Court stated that the hearing on the general is currently set for November 3, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.
